entities.name: PHAEOISARIOPSIS PERSONATA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen, also known as Mycosphaerella berkeleyi, that produces conidia on groundnut

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The host plant used in the study for the production of conidia by P. personata

entities.name: CONIDIAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The production of conidia by the fungus P. personata, influenced by environmental factors

entities.name: HUMIDITY
entities.description: Relative humidity (RH) levels, which must be above a threshold of 94.5% for conidial production to occur

entities.name: LEAF WETNESS
entities.description: The presence of water on the leaf surface, which influences the rate of conidial production

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The ambient temperature, which shows a linear increase in conidial production between 10 and 28°C

entities.name: LIGHT
entities.description: Light conditions including photon flux density and photoperiod that regulate conidial production

entities.name: CONTROLLED ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The use of controlled environmental conditions to isolate the effects of humidity, light, and temperature

relationships.description: positively correlates with production above 94.5% RH
influences the amount of conidia produced
positively correlates with production between 10 and 28°C
inhibits production at high photon flux density and influences biological rhythm
